title: Antifungal activity of momilactone A and B against rice fungal pathogens
abstract: Momilactones A and B, diterpene phytoalexins produced by rice (Oryza sativa), were tested for their inhibitory activity against the rice blast fungus Magnaporthe oryzae and the rice sheath blight fungus Rhizoctonia solani. Both compounds significantly suppressed mycelial growth of M. oryzae at concentrations above 50 μg/mL. Only momilactone A showed inhibitory activity against R. solani; momilactone B was inactive at all concentrations tested. These findings support the role of momilactones as antimicrobial defence compounds in rice and reveal differential specificity between the two congeners.
